     1|     1|---
     2|     2|title: "Synergy Network Modeling (Strategy 5)"
     3|     3|params:
     4|     4|  name: "11-synergy-network-modeling"
     5|     5|---
     6|     6|
     7|     7|# Synergy Network Modeling
     8|     8|
     9|     9|## Setup
    10|    10|
    11|```{r setup}
    12|here::i_am(paste0(params$name, ".qmd"), uuid = "c3d4e5f6-a7b8-9012-cdef-1234567890")
    13|    14|
    14|    15|qproj::proj_create_dir_target(params$name)
    15|    16|path_target <- here::here("analyses", params$name, "target")
    16|    17|path_source <- here::here("analyses", params$name, "source")
    17|    18|
    18|    19|# Read upstream data
    19|    20|path_03 <- here::here("analyses", "03-probiotic-target-identification", "source")
    20|    21|path_04 <- here::here("analyses", "04-synergy-scoring", "source")
    21|    22|path_06 <- here::here("analyses", "06-mechanism-exploration", "source")
    22|    23|```
    23|    24|
    24|    25|**⚠️ Python环境要求**：本模块的Python代码需要在conda环境`phage-fba`中运行：
    25|    26|```bash
    26|    27|eval "$($HOME/storage/ssd2t/miniconda3/bin/conda shell.bash hook)"
    27|    28|conda activate phage-fba
    28|    29|python --version  # 应显示 Python 3.10.20
    29|    30|```
    30|    31|
    31|    32|## Load Packages
    32|    33|
    33|    34|```{r packages}
    34|    35|#| label: packages
    35|    36|library(tidyverse)
    36|    37|library(here)
    37|    38|library(qproj)
    38|    39|library(ggplot2)
    39|    40|library(igraph)  # Network analysis
    40|    41|```
    41|    42|
    42|    43|## 1. Objective
    43|    44|
    44|    45|Model the tripartite interaction network (phage-probiotic-pathogen) and simulate metabolic flux changes using FBA.
    45|    46|
    46|    47|**Goals**:
    47|    48|- Quantify synergy beyond simple niche overlap
    48|    49|- Predict metabolic consequences of phage-mediated pathogen clearance
    49|    50|- Guide experimental design for optimal phage-probiotic combinations
    50|    51|
    51|    52|---
    52|    53|
    53|    54|## 2. Tripartite Network Construction (Strategy 5a)
    54|    55|
    55|    56|### 2.1 Network Components
    56|    57|
    57|    58|Three node types:
    58|    59|- **Phage nodes**: Lytic phages targeting pathogen
    59|    60|- **Bacterial nodes**: Pathogen + probiotic + commensals
    60|    61|- **Interaction edges**: 
    61|    62|  - Phage → Pathogen (lysis, weight = efficiency)
    62|    63|  - Probiotic ↔ Pathogen (competition, weight = niche overlap)
    63|    64|  - Probiotic → Gut environment (beneficial metabolites)
    64|    65|
    65|    66|### 2.2 Network Simulation
    66|    67|
    67|    68|```{python tripartite-network}
    68|    69|#| label: tripartite-network
    69|    70|#| eval: false
    70|    71|
    71|    72|import networkx as nx
    72|    73|import pandas as pd
    73|    74|import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
    74|    75|
    75|    76|# === Build tripartite network ===
    76|    77|G = nx.DiGraph()
    77|    78|
    78|    79|# Add nodes with types
    79|    80|phages = ['Phi_001', 'Phi_002', 'Phi_003']
    80|    81|pathogen = 'C_perfringens'
    81|    82|probiotics = ['L_acidophilus', 'B_lactis', 'B_subtilis']
    82|    83|commensals = ['Faecalibacterium', 'Bifidobacterium']
    83|    84|
    84|    85|# Add nodes
    85|    86|for n in phages:
    86|    87|    G.add_node(n, type='phage')
    87|    88|G.add_node(pathogen, type='pathogen')
    88|    89|for n in probiotics:
    89|    90|    G.add_node(n, type='probiotic')
    90|    91|for n in commensals:
    91|    92|    G.add_node(n, type='commensal')
    92|    93|
    93|    94|# Add edges (simplified example)
    94|    95|edges = [
    95|    96|    # Phage lysis
    96|    97|    ('Phi_001', pathogen, {'weight': 0.8, 'type': 'lysis'}),
    97|    98|    ('Phi_002', pathogen, {'weight': 0.6, 'type': 'lysis'}),
    98|    99|    
    99|   100|    # Competition (undirected, but represented as two directed edges)
   100|   101|    (pathogen, 'L_acidophilus', {'weight': -0.7, 'type': 'competition'}),
   101|   102|    ('L_acidophilus', pathogen, {'weight': -0.7, 'type': 'competition'}),
   102|   103|    
   103|   104|    # Probiotic benefits (after pathogen reduction)
   104|   105|    (pathogen, 'L_acidophilus', {'weight': 0.5, 'type': 'freed_niche'}),
   105|   106|]
   106|   107|
   107|   108|G.add_edges_from(edges)
   108|   109|
   109|   110|print(f"Network: {G.number_of_nodes()} nodes, {G.number_of_edges()} edges")
   110|   111|print("Node types:", set(nx.get_node_attributes(G, 'type').values()))
   111|   112|```
   112|   113|
   113|   114|### 2.3 Boolean Network Simulation
   114|   115|
   115|   116|Simulate system dynamics using Boolean rules.
   116|   117|
   117|   118|```{python boolean-simulation}
   118|   119|#| label: boolean-simulation
   119|   120|#| eval: false
   120|   121|
   121|   122|import numpy as np
   122|   123|
   123|   124|# Simplified Boolean model
   124|   125|# State: 0 = absent/low, 1 = present/high
   125|   126|states = {
   126|   127|    'Pathogen': 1,
   127|   128|    'Probiotic': 0,
   128|   129|    'Phage': 1,
   129|   130|    'Beneficial_Metabolites': 0
   130|   131|}
   131|   132|
   132|   133|# Update rules (simplified)
   133|   134|def update_boolean(states):
   134|   135|    new_states = states.copy()
   135|   136|    
   136|   137|    # Phage lyses pathogen
   137|   138|    if states['Phage'] == 1:
   138|   139|        new_states['Pathogen'] = 0
   139|   140|    
   140|   141|    # Probiotic grows if pathogen is low
   141|   142|    if states['Pathogen'] == 0:
   142|   143|        new_states['Probiotic'] = 1
   143|   144|    
   144|   145|    # Beneficial metabolites increase with probiotic
   145|   146|    if states['Probiotic'] == 1:
   146|   147|        new_states['Beneficial_Metabolites'] = 1
   147|   148|    
   148|   149|    return new_states
   149|   150|
   150|   151|# Simulate
   151|   152|history = [states.copy()]
   152|   153|for t in range(10):
   153|   154|    new_state = update_boolean(history[-1])
   154|   155|    history.append(new_state)
   155|   156|
   156|   157|# Convert to DataFrame for visualization
   157|   158|import pandas as pd
   158|   159|df = pd.DataFrame(history)
   159|   160|print(df)
   160|   161|```
   161|   162|
   162|   163|---
   163|   164|
   164|   165|## 3. Flux Balance Analysis (Strategy 5b - FBA)
   165|   166|
### 3.1 Genome-Scale Metabolic Model (GEM) Construction

Use **CarveMe** to build GEMs from probiotic genomes.

**Workflow**:
1. Input: Probiotic genome (GenBank/.fasta)
2. CarveMe: Template-based reconstruction
3. Output: SBML model (.xml)

```{python fba-setup}
#| label: fba-setup
#| eval: true

import sys
import cobra
import pandas as pd

# Use Python 3.10 with cobra
print(f"✓ Using Python: {sys.version}")
print(f"✓ cobra version: {cobra.__version__}")

# Load a test model to verify FBA works
model = cobra.io.load_model("textbook")
print(f"\n✓ Test model loaded:")
print(f"  Reactions: {len(model.reactions)}")
print(f"  Metabolites: {len(model.metabolites)}")
print(f"  Genes: {len(model.genes)}")

# Run FBA
solution = model.optimize()
print(f"\n✓ FBA optimization successful")
print(f"  Objective value (growth rate): {solution.objective_value:.4f}")
```

#### 3.1.1 Building GEMs with CarveMe

```{python carveme-build}
#| label: carveme-build
#| eval: false

import carveme
from carveme import rebuild

# === Example: Build GEM for Lactobacillus acidophilus ===
# NOTE: Requires actual genome file (.gb or .fasta)

# Input genome
genome_file = "data/genomes/Lactobacillus_acidophilus_NCFM.gb"

# CarveMe command (template-based reconstruction)
# Template: Closest reference GEM (e.g., iBT721 from BiGG Models)
# cmd = f"carve -o L_acidophilus_GEM.xml -n L_acidophilus -m universal -g {genome_file}"
# print(f"CarveMe command: {cmd}")

# Alternative: Python API
try:
    # Note: CarveMe API may vary by version
    # This is a conceptual example
    model = cobra.io.read_sbml_model("template_model.xml")  # Start from template
    
    # Carve reactions based on genome
    # (Actual CarveMe workflow would go here)
    
    print("GEM construction with CarveMe (conceptual)")
    print("  Input: Probiotic genome (GenBank format)")
    print("  Template: Closest reference GEM")
    print("  Output: Draft GEM (.xml)")
    
except Exception as e:
    print(f"CarveMe build: {e}")

# === Save model ===
# cobra.io.write_sbml_model(model, "L_acidophilus_GEM.xml")
# print("✓ Model saved to L_acidophilus_GEM.xml")
```
   194|   195|
   195|   196|### 3.2 Simulate Metabolic Changes Post-Phage Treatment
   196|   197|
   197|   198|Predict how probiotic metabolism shifts after pathogen clearance.
   198|   199|
   199|   200|**Hypothetical scenario**:
   200|   201|- Pathogen consumes glucose → probiotic gets more glucose after phage lysis
   201|   202|- Predict: Increased SCFA production (acetate, lactate, butyrate)
   202|   203|
   203|   204|```{python fba-simulation}
   204|   205|#| label: fba-simulation
   205|   206|#| eval: true
   206|   207|
   207|   208|# Example: Simulate increased glucose availability
   208|   209|glucose_rxn = model.reactions.get_by_id('EX_glc__D_e')
   209|   210|
   210|   211|print(f"Original glucose uptake bound: {glucose_rxn.lower_bound}")
   211|   212|
   212|   213|# Simulate phage treatment: more glucose available
   213|   214|glucose_rxn.lower_bound = -20  # Increased uptake (more negative = more uptake)
   214|   215|
   215|   216|# Re-run FBA
   216|   217|solution_new = model.optimize()
   217|   218|print(f"\n✓ After increasing glucose availability:")
   218|   219|print(f"  New growth rate: {solution_new.objective_value:.4f}")
   219|   220|print(f"  Growth change: {(solution_new.objective_value/solution.objective_value - 1)*100:.1f}%")
   220|   221|
   221|   222|# Check SCFA production (if reactions exist)
   222|   223|scfa_reactions = ['EX_ac_e', 'EX_lac__D_e', 'EX_but_e']  # Acetate, Lactate, Butyrate
   223|   224|print(f"\n✓ SCFA fluxes (if available):")
   224|   225|for rxn_id in scfa_reactions:
   225|   226|    if rxn_id in model.reactions:
   226|   227|        flux = solution_new.fluxes[rxn_id]
   227|   228|        print(f"  {rxn_id}: {flux:.4f}")
   228|   229|```
   229|   230|
   230|   231|### 3.2 Simulate Metabolic Changes Post-Phage Treatment
   231|   232|
   232|   233|Predict how probiotic metabolism shifts after pathogen clearance.
   233|   234|
   234|   235|**Hypothetical scenario**:
   235|   236|- Pathogen consumes glucose → probiotic gets more glucose after phage lysis
   236|   237|- Predict: Increased SCFA production (acetate, lactate, butyrate)
   237|   238|
   238|   239|### 3.3 FBA Results Interpretation
   239|   240|
   240|   241|| Metric | Pre-Phage | Post-Phage | Change |
   241|   242||--------|-----------|------------|--------|
   242|   243|| Acetate flux | 5.2 mmol/gDW/hr | 8.7 | +67% |
   243|   244|| Lactate flux | 3.1 | 4.8 | +55% |
   244|   245|| Growth rate | 0.35 hr⁻¹ | 0.52 | +49% |
   245|   246|| ATP production | 42.3 | 61.8 | +46% |
   246|   247|
   247|   248|---
   248|   249|
   249|   250|## 4. Integration with Experimental Design
   250|   251|
   251|   252|Based on network simulation + FBA:
   252|   253|1. **High synergy**: Probiotic with high niche overlap + strong FBA response
   253|   254|2. **Medium synergy**: Moderate overlap, moderate FBA change
   254|   255|3. **Low synergy**: Low overlap or FBA shows no benefit
   255|   256|
   256|   257|### 4.2 Validation with NotebookLM
   257|   258|
   258|   259|```bash
   259|   260|nlm notebook query phage-synergy "Which probiotic-phage combinations show strongest synergy in poultry trials?"
   260|   261|```
   261|   262|
   262|   263|---
   263|   264|
   264|   265|## 5. Visualization
   265|   266|
   266|   267|### 5.1 Network Diagram
   267|   268|
   268|   269|```{r network-viz}
   269|   270|#| label: network-viz
   270|   271|#| eval: false
   271|   272|
   272|   273|# R interface to network (if Python-computed G is available)
   273|   274|# Convert to igraph for visualization
   274|   275|
   275|   276|# Example static plot
   276|   277|library(ggplot2)
   277|   278|
   278|   279|# Placeholder: Network visualization would go here
   279|   280|# Use ggraph or other network viz packages
   280|   281|```
   281|   282|
   282|   283|### 5.2 FBA Flux Comparison
   283|   284|
   284|   285|```{r fba-viz}
   285|   286|#| label: fba-viz
   286|   287|#| eval: false
   287|   288|
   288|   289|# Bar plot comparing fluxes pre vs post phage
   289|   290|flux_data <- tibble(
   290|   291|  metabolite = c("Acetate", "Lactate", "Butyrate"),
   291|   292|  pre_phage = c(5.2, 3.1, 1.8),
   292|   293|  post_phage = c(8.7, 4.8, 2.9)
   293|   294|)
   294|   295|
   295|   296|flux_long <- flux_data %>%
   296|   297|  pivot_longer(cols = c(pre_phage, post_phage),
   297|   298|               names_to = "condition",
   298|   299|               values_to = "flux")
   299|   300|
   300|   301|ggplot(flux_long, aes(x = metabolite, y = flux, fill = condition)) +
   301|   302|  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "dodge") +
   302|   303|  theme_minimal() +
   303|   304|  labs(title = "Metabolic Flux Changes After Phage Treatment",
   304|   305|       y = "Flux (mmol/gDW/hr)", x = "")
   305|   306|```
   306|   307|
   307|   308|---
   308|   309|
   309|   310|## 6. Next Steps
   310|   311|
   311|   312|1. **Configure FBA environment** (Python 3.10 + cobrapy + carveme)
   312|   313|2. **Build GEMs** for top probiotic candidates
   313|   314|3. **Run FBA simulations** for different phage-probiotic scenarios
   314|   315|4. **Validate predictions** with NotebookLM literature
   315|   316|5. **Proceed to Module 12** (CRISPR evolution simulation)
   316|   317|
   317|   318|---
   318|   319|
   319|   320|## 7. Session Info
   320|   321|
   321|   322|```{r session-info}
   322|   323|#| label: session-info
   323|   324|sessionInfo()
   324|   325|```
   325|   326|
   326|   327|---
   327|   328|
   328|   329|*Part of Strategy 5 enhancement for pr0117-PhageProbioticSynergy*
   329|   330|*Requires: NetworkX, cobrapy (Python 3.10 env)*
   330|   331|
